title: हरि स्तोत्रम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री हरि स्तोत्रम् ॥

जगज्जालपालं कचत्कन्धरालं
शरच्चन्द्रभालं महादैत्यकालम्।
नभोनीलकायं दुरावारमायं
सुपद्मासहायं भजेऽहं भजेऽहम्॥ १॥

सदाम्भोधिवासं गलत्पुष्पहासं
जगत्सन्निवासं शतादित्यभासम्।
गदाचक्रशस्त्रं लसत्पीतवस्त्रं
हसच्चारुवक्त्रं भजेऽहं भजेऽहम्॥ २॥

रमाकन्ठहारं श्रुतिव्रातसारं
जलान्तर्विहारं धराभारहारम्।
चिदानन्दरूपं मनोज्ञस्वरूपं
धृतानेकरूपं भजेऽहं भजेऽहम्॥ ३॥

जराजन्मनाशं परापापनाशं
समस्ताघनाशं सकामार्तिनाशम्।
धनं जन्मनाशं सुखाकारमीशं
विधित्सालसं तं भजेऽहं भजेऽहम्॥ ४॥

नभोनीलदेहं बृहत्कीर्तिगेहं
नमत्सर्वदेहं विपद्वैरिदेहम्।
परं प्राणनाथं जगन्नाथनाथं
सनातनेशं भजेऽहं भजेऽहम्॥ ५॥

सशंखं सचक्रं सकीरीटकुण्डलं
सपीतं सकौस्तुभं वनमालिनम्।
रमासेवितं तं महाविष्णुमीशं
सदा शुद्धसत्त्वं भजेऽहं भजेऽहम्॥ ६॥

सुरारातिवक्त्रं तथा वारणक्त्रं
महादैत्यदन्तं सदा वेदवन्तम्।
जगज्जीवहेतुं सकृद्विश्वसेतुं
महोदारचित्तं भजेऽहं भजेऽहम्॥ ७॥

महाम्भोधिमध्ये विराजन्तमेकं
विलासोल्लसद्दिव्यरत्नं विचित्रम्।
महाशेषतल्पे सुखासीनमारा-
द्भजामोऽनुगं हं भजेऽहं भजेऽहम्॥ ८॥

॥ इति श्रीमच्छंकराचार्यविरचितं श्री हरि स्तोत्रम् सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Hari Stotram ||

Jagaj-jala-palam kachat-kandhara-alam
Sharach-chandra-bhalam maha-daitya-kalam.
Nabho-nila-kayam duravara-mayam
Su-padma-sahayam bhaje-aham bhaje-aham. || 1 ||

Sada-ambhodhi-vasam galat-pushpa-hasam
Jagat-sannivasam shata-aditya-bhasam.
Gada-chakra-shastram lasat-pita-vastram
Hasach-charu-vaktram bhaje-aham bhaje-aham. || 2 ||

Rama-kantha-haram shruti-vrata-saram
Jala-antar-viharam dhara-bhara-haram.
Chid-ananda-rupam manojnya-svarupam
Dhrita-aneka-rupam bhaje-aham bhaje-aham. || 3 ||

Jara-janma-nasham para-apa-nasham
Samasta-agha-nasham sa-kama-arti-nasham.
Dhanam janma-nasham sukha-akaram-isham
Vidhitsa-lasam tam bhaje-aham bhaje-aham. || 4 ||

Nabho-nila-deham brihat-kirti-geham
Namat-sarva-deham vipad-vairi-deham.
Param prana-natham jagan-natha-natham
Sanatana-isham bhaje-aham bhaje-aham. || 5 ||

Sa-shankham sa-chakram sa-kirita-kundalam
Sa-pitam sa-kaustubham vana-malinam.
Rama-sevitam tam Maha-Vishnum-isham
Sada shuddha-sattvam bhaje-aham bhaje-aham. || 6 ||

Sura-arati-vaktram tatha varana-aktram
Maha-daitya-dantam sada veda-vantam.
Jagaj-jiva-hetum sakrid-vishva-setum
Maha-udara-chittam bhaje-aham bhaje-aham. || 7 ||

Maha-ambhodhi-madhye virajantam-ekam
Vilasa-ullasad-divya-ratnam vichitram.
Maha-shesha-talpe sukha-asinam-ara-
Ad-bhajamo-anugam ham bhaje-aham bhaje-aham. || 8 ||

|| Iti Shrimach-Shankaracharya-Virachitam Shri Hari Stotram Sampurnam ||
hindiMeaning:
जगत् रूपी जाल के पालनकर्ता, चमकते कण्ठाभरण वाले, शरद् चन्द्रमा के समान ललाट वाले, महादैत्यों के काल, आकाश के समान नीलकाय, दुर्निवार माया वाले, पद्मा (लक्ष्मी) सहित — उन्हें मैं भजता हूँ, मैं भजता हूँ॥ १॥

सदा समुद्र में निवास करने वाले, खिले पुष्प की भाँति हँसने वाले, जगत् में संनिवास करने वाले, सौ सूर्यों की भाँति प्रभा वाले, गदा-चक्र शस्त्र धारण करने वाले, चमकते पीत वस्त्र वाले, हँसते सुन्दर मुख वाले — उन्हें मैं भजता हूँ॥ २॥

रमा (लक्ष्मी) के कण्ठ के हार, वेदसमूहों के सार, जल के भीतर विहार करने वाले, पृथ्वी का भार हरने वाले, चिदानन्द रूप, मनोज्ञ स्वरूप, अनेक रूप धारण करने वाले — उन्हें मैं भजता हूँ॥ ३॥

जरा, जन्म, पाप, समस्त अघ, काम-पीड़ा का नाश करने वाले, सुख के आकार, ईश — उन्हें मैं भजता हूँ॥ ४॥

शंख, चक्र, किरीट, कुण्डल, पीताम्बर, कौस्तुभमणि, वनमाला धारण करने वाले, रमा (लक्ष्मी) द्वारा सेवित, शुद्धसत्त्वमय महाविष्णु ईश को मैं सदा भजता हूँ॥ ६॥

महासागर के मध्य में विराजमान, विलास से प्रकाशित दिव्य रत्नों से विचित्र, महाशेष तल्प पर सुखपूर्वक विराजमान — उनको हम भजते हैं, मैं भजता हूँ॥ ८॥
benefits: Composed by Adi Shankaracharya — a beautiful rhythmic praise of Vishnu
The refrain 'Bhaje Aham Bhaje Aham' creates deep devotional absorption
Traditionally recited for removal of old age, rebirth, sins, and all sorrows
Invokes the magnificent form of Vishnu reclining on Shesha
Invokes peace, devotion, and spiritual joy
Ideal for both Advaita and Bhakti practitioners
description: Hari Stotram — हरि स्तोत्रम् — is a devotional hymn of praise offered to Lord Vishnu. This revered text originates from Adi Shankaracharya. Regular recitation is believed to invoke the magnificent form of Vishnu reclining on Shesha, composed by Adi Shankaracharya — a beautiful rhythmic praise of Vishnu, and destroy old age, rebirth, sins, and all sorrows. This prayer is traditionally recited on Thursday (Guruvar) and Sunday (Ravivar) and during Ekadashi and Vaikuntha Ekadashi to invoke divine grace. At 8 verses long, the recitation takes roughly 6 minutes, making it accessible for regular devotional use. On this page, you can read the complete Hari Stotram in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Hari Stotram by Adi Shankaracharya in Sanskrit with Hindi meaning. Melodious hymn with 'Bhaje Aham' refrain glorifying Lord Vishnu.
relatedSearches: Sri Hari Stotram lyrics in Sanskrit
Vishnu morning prayer for daily recitation
Difference between Hari Stotram and Hari Nama Mala

seoFaqs.question: Is Hari Stotram authored by Adi Shankaracharya or someone else?
seoFaqs.answer: Sri Hari Stotram, opening with the famous line Jagajjalapalam Chalatkanthamalam, is most commonly attributed to Swami Brahmananda, although several online sources also credit Adi Shankaracharya. The hymn does not appear in the standard Shankara Granthavali compiled by tradition, and most scholarly sources accept the Brahmananda attribution. Whoever the composer, the verses are unmistakably Vedantic in flavour, presenting Hari as the cosmic sustainer who is simultaneously the personal beloved of devotees, a non-dual reading characteristic of post-Shankara Smarta Vaishnavism. The misattribution arose because the hymn shares the philosophical tone of Shankara's other Vishnu stotras.

seoFaqs.question: What does the opening phrase Jagajjalapalam mean?
seoFaqs.answer: Jagajjala-palam is a compound meaning sustainer (pala) of the net or web (jala) of the world (jagat), evoking the image of Vishnu as the holder of the entire cosmic web in his hands. The phrase is paired with chalatkanthamalam, the one whose neck-garland sways gently, suggesting both grandeur and intimate beauty. Together the opening line presents Hari simultaneously as transcendent cosmic sustainer and as the gracefully ornamented blue-bodied Lord who is dear to his devotees. This pairing of cosmic and personal is the central poetic device of the entire stotram, repeated in different images across each verse.

seoFaqs.question: How many verses does Sri Hari Stotram contain?
seoFaqs.answer: The hymn most commonly circulates as an ashtakam, that is, eight verses, with a closing phalashruti that brings the total to nine recited stanzas. Some printed editions add additional verses or pair the stotram with the related Hari Stuti, which can lead to confusion about its length, but the canonical core is eight verses. Each verse is dense with compound Sanskrit epithets that pile image upon image: Hari as ocean-dweller, sky-blue Lord of unfathomable maya, husband of Lakshmi, bridge across the three worlds, slayer of demons, and the very breath of his devotees. The compactness combined with imagistic richness is what gives the hymn its enduring popularity.

seoFaqs.question: What is the recommended fruit of daily recitation according to the phalashruti?
seoFaqs.answer: The closing verse declares that one who recites this Hari Stotram daily with a concentrated mind will, without doubt, attain the sorrowless state of Vishnu, that is, the param padam or supreme abode beyond the cycle of birth and death. Commentators explain this as both a long-term promise of moksha and a near-term fruit of mental tranquillity, since the very act of contemplating Hari's expansive cosmic form draws the mind out of its small anxieties. Many devotees report that the rhythmic Sanskrit metre itself induces a meditative state when the stotram is chanted slowly, even before the meaning of the verses is fully grasped.

seoFaqs.question: When is Sri Hari Stotram traditionally chanted?
seoFaqs.answer: It is recited as a daily morning or evening prayer in many Vaishnava households, on Ekadashi and Dwadashi as part of fasting observances, on Vaikuntha Ekadashi (the Margashirsha shukla ekadashi when the gates of Vaikuntha are said to open), on the four months of Chaturmasya, and during temple darshanas of Vishnu. The hymn is also widely chanted at funerals and shraddha ceremonies as a prayer for the departed soul to attain Vishnu's abode. Its short length, eight verses, makes it suitable for inclusion alongside longer texts such as the Vishnu Sahasranama and Bhagavad Gita chapter 15 (Purushottama Yoga).

seoFaqs.question: What makes the Sanskrit of Hari Stotram considered especially beautiful?
seoFaqs.answer: Sanskrit critics often highlight three qualities. First, the long compound words (samasa) pack four or five descriptive ideas into a single rhythmic unit, demanding focus from the reciter and rewarding it with vivid imagery. Second, the metre is the slow and stately Bhujangaprayata, which suits a hymn meant to evoke the majesty of the cosmic Lord. Third, the verses use sound symbolism: the soft m and l consonants in Jagajjalapalam Chalatkanthamalam evoke the gentle swaying motion of Vishnu's garland, while the harsher consonants in verses about defeating demons evoke martial energy. This careful sonic engineering is one reason the hymn endures as both prayer and poetry.
